articles.title: ‘Main Street vs The Merger’: Hollywood workers rally against $110 bn Paramount-Warner deal

Concerns are high for creative talent and behind-the-scenes workers facing declining employment.

articles.title: ‘Khan Sir Won't Surrender In Patna Court, To File Anticipatory Bail Plea On 8 June’: Lawyer
articles.description: Educationist and coaching institute owner Faisal Khan, widely known as Khan Sir, will not surrender before the Patna Civil Court in connection with a case linked to the June 2 attack on his coaching centre, according to his lawyer Arvind Kumar Mavvar. The legal team plans to seek anticipatory bail, with the lawyer stating that the deadline to file the plea ended on Saturday and that the application would now be moved on Monday. The case relates to an attack on Khan Sir’s institute, where videos showed several individuals allegedly assaulting a security guard, pelting bricks and stones, and tearing down posters outside the premises. While reports initially suggested firing, Patna Police denied any gunfire. Two security guards employed at the institute have already been arrested and sent to jail, while Khan Sir was questioned during the investigation.

articles.title: Court protecting rights only for those who can litigate not fulfilling constitutional function: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant
articles.description: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that courts must be accessible to all citizens. He emphasized that justice is earned through transparency and self-correction. Technology can expand access to justice, making courts more efficient. The CJI also highlighted the challenge of volume and speed in the legal system. He believes justice lies in balancing law with human realities.

articles.title: Rs 1,100 for parathas, Rs 500 for 3 eggs: Man calls out 'absurd' food prices at luxury hotel, sparks debate
articles.description: When his dinner bill at a five-star hotel in Mumbai came to Rs 2,300, one guest simply could not believe it. His order of aloo parathas, boiled eggs, curd and a milkshake has since sparked a debate about luxury pricing.

articles.title: 'Mantri' Pradhan's continuation in office speaks of his desire for power: Congress on CBSE OSM row
articles.description: Congress criticizes Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the CBSE exam controversy. Allegations point to a compromised ministry and inflated rates for a new system. Students faced stress due to these issues. The minister's continuation in office is questioned. An independent probe is demanded. The CBSE extended the deadline for answer sheet verification.

articles.title: Four former AIADMK Ministers join TVK amid fresh wave of defections
articles.description: Four former AIADMK Ministers defect to Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, strengthening the ruling party amid ongoing political shifts.

articles.title: BNP protests against Pakistan govt over enforced disappearances, extrajudicial killings
articles.description: Balochistan National Party (BNP) is protesting across Quetta and Balochistan, alleging escalating state repression including enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings. Demonstrations followed a raid on senior leader Sardar Naseer Ahmed Musiyani's home, which reportedly resulted in his son's death and several detentions. BNP demands an independent probe and an end to targeted violence.

articles.title: Word of the Day: Accismus
articles.description: Accismus describes the act of pretending to reject something you secretly want. The rhetorical term reveals how modesty, image, and hidden desire shape human behavior.
